other_observers: Brandon Percival refound this bird off from the small island in the southeastern-most part of Pueblo Reservoir.; Brandon Percival first found this bird on the 13th of December in the same spot.
description: This is a small-billed, round-headed gull with yellow legs about the size of or a little smaller than a Ring-billed Gull.  The very thin yellow bill with a very thin grayish mark at the gonys, small brownish eye was evident. The bird was seen in the water and flying. On the water the mantle color was noticeably darker than nearby Ring-billed Gulls, the tertial and scapular white crescents stood out, especially the significant width of the tertail crescent. Gray spotting was dense and confined to the crown and nape, abscent from the sides of the face. The bill was distinctly yellowish with only a small dark gash running up from the gonys. Bill was shorter than that of RBGU.  In flight the most noticeable feature was the two large white mirrors on P9 and P10. A faint "string of pearls" could be seen contrasting with the otherwise dark (except the white mirrors) wingtips. These "pearls" extended along the primaries from about p10 through p5 and then quite visible was a wide white trailing edge to the flight feathers. A heavily spotted and darkish hindneck stood out from the rest of the head and the tail appeared all-white.  The head and neck were less densly spotted than RBGUs and the head appeared shorted and quite rounded (See photo)Behaviors: Mostly sitting on the water. When in flight, made erractic tacks to the wind dropping to the water three times and then taking flight again after each descent to the water.Call: nonePlumage: adult winter
